Find Sherpas Boutique Search Agency
Search Quality Report

60 Lyko Searches. 27 Revenue-Risk Moments.

An independent audit of how Lyko search handles product names, brands, filters, and shopper intent.
Prepared by Michal Pekarcik · Find Sherpas
Report Crossroads

Jump to a section

Search risk at a glance → Tested query patterns → Improvement map → Test results summary → Advanced diagnostics → Prioritized roadmap → Revenue-risk failures → Detailed analysis → Complete test results → Scope and boundaries →
Summary

Search risk at a glance

The short version

We tested 60 realistic customer queries against Lyko's search, live on site. The audit checks whether customers see a relevant top result, not just whether the search engine returns something.

This report focuses on the 27 queries where a customer likely hits a dead end: an off-topic result, a missing interpretation, a dropped constraint, or a relevant product buried too far down.

The first fix to ship: add an intent layer before ranking, so sale, bestseller, price, negative, and brand terms act as filters or boosts before generic keyword matches take over.

45%
revenue risk. 27 of 60 tested queries cause a likely customer bounce today. These are queries where a customer sees a dead end: no relevant top result, wrong interpretation, or the right product buried too far down.
27
Revenue risk
Off-topic top result, missing interpretation, or severe ranking failure. The customer leaves.
32
Friction
The query is close, but still creates work: the best match is not surfaced cleanly enough.
1
Working well
Top result is relevant and ranking is near-optimal. No immediate attention needed.
95%
Right products found, ranked too low
#6
Avg. best result position
63%
Best result outside top 3
18%
Irrelevant #1 result
Most severe example A customer searching "läppbalsam" sees "Ole Henriksen Pout Preserve Peptide Lip Treatment Strawberry Sortbet" at the #1 spot — not what they asked for.
Pattern Calibration Map

Where each query family needs tuning

These are diagnostic probes, not grades. Each row shows the strength of the tuning signal in that sample, separating severe shopper-visible misses from minor ranking polish.

Product Discovery
Direct match
Exact-match polish
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss; the examples below show where the behavior breaks.
Can customers find an exact product when they already know the name?
Next tuning move: Protect exact product-title matches and verify they are not outranked by broader keyword matches.
Product Discovery
Broad category
Collection routing
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 1 friction point, 2 polish notes.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss; the examples below show where the behavior breaks.
Does search understand high-level shopping terms and collection-style queries?
Next tuning move: Map common retail terms to collections, category pages, and merchandising rules.
Typo Tolerance
Typo
Typo recovery
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 2 friction points, 1 stable result.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss; the examples below show where the behavior breaks.
Does search recover when brand or product terms are misspelled?
Next tuning move: Add fuzzy matching for high-value brands, product names, and common category terms.
Typo Tolerance
Merged words
Pattern tuning
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 1 friction point, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss; the examples below show where the behavior breaks.
This probe checks whether search handles this customer query pattern predictably.
Next tuning move: Review the failed examples and add this pattern to the search relevance regression set.

Split word
Pattern tuning
4 probes reviewed: 3 friction points, 1 polish note.
The pattern works in places, but the experience is not consistently clean.

Next tuning move: Review the failed examples and add this pattern to the search relevance regression set.

Special character
Pattern tuning
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 1 friction point, 1 polish note.

Typo Tolerance
Partial query
Autocomplete intent
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 friction point.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss; the examples below show where the behavior breaks.
Do incomplete typed-ahead queries surface plausible products before the full term is entered?
Next tuning move: Tune prefix matching and autocomplete-backed retrieval for short or incomplete terms.
Language Understanding
Synonym
Vocabulary mapping
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 2 friction points.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss; the examples below show where the behavior breaks.
Does search connect customer vocabulary to the store's preferred product wording?
Next tuning move: Expand synonym dictionaries and semantic embeddings for the product language customers actually use.
Brand & Model Search
Brand search
Brand protection
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 friction point.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss; the examples below show where the behavior breaks.
Are brand requests recognized and protected from unrelated results?
Next tuning move: Detect brand entities and boost or filter to products from the requested brand.

Plural / singular
Term normalization

Does search normalize simple wording variants such as singular and plural terms?
Next tuning move: Normalize singular, plural, and close inflection variants before retrieval.

Subjective attribute
Merchandising language

Filters & Constraints
Multi-attribute
Constraint stacking
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 1 friction point, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss; the examples below show where the behavior breaks.
Can search keep multiple requirements active at the same time?
Next tuning move: Score each constraint independently so one matched term does not drown out the rest.
Improvement Map

Where search can work harder

Focus Capability What it means
Focus area Product Discovery 8 of 8 query patterns show improvement room. This is a practical place to start for Product Discovery.
Focus area Typo Tolerance 19 of 20 query patterns show improvement room. This is a practical place to start for Typo Tolerance.
Focus area Language Understanding 4 of 4 query patterns show improvement room. This is a practical place to start for Language Understanding.
Focus area Brand & Model Search 8 of 8 query patterns show improvement room. This is a practical place to start for Brand & Model Search.
Focus area Shopping Context 16 of 16 query patterns show improvement room. This is a practical place to start for Shopping Context.
Focus area Filters & Constraints 4 of 4 query patterns show improvement room. This is a practical place to start for Filters & Constraints.
Use this as an improvement map: the focus areas are where changes are likely to help the most customer journeys.
Prism Test Results

60 queries tested

SeverityQueries
Critical27
Moderate18
Minor14
Pass1

Failure Mode Breakdown

Failure ModeQueries
Poor ranking57
Other1
No semantic understanding1
  • #6 average best result position
  • 95% of queries: retrieval OK but ranking failed (57 of 60)
  • 63% of queries had their best result outside the top 3
  • 18% of queries showed an irrelevant result in the top position
Advanced Diagnostics

What the visible result set reveals

These checks stay inside the same query → results → audit boundary, but look beyond the single best result.

Result Set Purity

This checks how much of the visible result set is actually relevant, not just whether one good result exists somewhere. A noisy top 5 means customers must visually filter around weak results even when search technically found a match.

43%
top 5 relevant

Result

The visible result set is noisy: only 43% of top 5 results were relevant on average, and top 10 purity was 45%.

Evidence

  • "balsam": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "smink": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "naglar": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "olaples": 0/5 relevant in top 5

How to remedy it

Tune ranking to demote low-relevance partial matches once a strong intent match exists. Add a top-N quality guardrail: for each important query class, require a minimum share of relevant results in the top 5, not only a good best-result position.

Top Result Trust

This asks whether the first result is defensible as the best thing to show a customer. A bad #1 is the clearest search failure because it shapes the shopper's first impression.

58%
questionable #1 results

Result

The first result is not reliable enough: 35 of 60 queries (58%) had a weak, partial, or clearly beaten #1 result.

Evidence

  • "hem & spa": #1 was "Sponsrad Marie-Stella-Maris Fragrance Sticks Violet Muse 250 ml" (score 0.25). The first result is weakly related to the query.
  • "par fym": #1 was "Sponsrad Dove Whole Body Deodorant Lavender & Chamomile Deodorant Stick 75 ml" (score 0.29). The first result is weakly related to the query.
  • "hälsa & välmående": #1 was "Red Bull Blue Edition 250 ml" (score 0.29). The first result is weakly related to the query.
  • "naglar": #1 was "Depend Gel Builder Soft Pink" (score 0.29). The first result is weakly related to the query.

How to remedy it

Add a stricter first-result threshold for high-intent queries. If the top result is weak and a stronger result exists lower, boost the stronger exact/category/brand match or suppress the weak partial match from rank #1.

Attribute Drift

This decomposes multi-attribute queries to see which terms survive into the top results. It distinguishes right-category-but-wrong-attribute failures from total search failures.

4
queries with attribute loss

Result

4 multi-attribute queries showed some attribute loss in the top 5. The audit separates preserved, partially preserved, and dropped terms so the team can see which part of the query is being lost.

Evidence

  • "cerave ansiktskräm känslig hud": dropped ansiktskr, nslig, hud; partially preserved none
  • "kérastase balsam skadad hår": dropped balsam, skadad; partially preserved none
  • "maybelline mascara svart vattentät": dropped svart, vattent; partially preserved none
  • "redken shampoo torrt hår": dropped torrt; partially preserved none

How to remedy it

Extract query attributes before ranking and give required attributes explicit weight. For brand, size, material, activity, and style terms, penalize results that match the product type but omit or contradict the attribute.

Diversity vs Redundancy

This checks whether broad or exploratory queries show useful choice, or whether the top results are flooded by repeated variants. It is most useful for multi-brand or broad-catalog ecommerce, and less useful for true single-brand stores.

9
unique families in worst broad top 10

Result

The most redundant broad result set was "herrprodukter": 9 unique product families across 10 top results.

Evidence

  • "herrprodukter": 9 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "smink": 10 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "naglar": 10 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "parfym": 10 unique product families across 10 top results

How to remedy it

Group color/size/variant duplicates under a product-family cap for broad queries. Keep exact product searches variant-rich, but diversify broad discovery queries across families, brands, styles, and use cases.

Query Specificity Scaling

This compares broad -> specific query chains to see whether search gets better as the shopper becomes clearer. High-specificity queries often signal stronger purchase intent, so degradation here is commercially important.

0
specificity ladders detected

Result

No three-step specificity ladder was detected in this query set. This diagnostic activates when generated queries include broad -> specific query chains.

How to remedy it

Generate intentional specificity ladders during query creation, then use them as regression tests. When a ladder breaks, inspect the attribute added at that step and tune query parsing or ranking weights for that attribute.

Recommendations

Prioritized Roadmap

1

Fix relevance score ranking across all search types

Reconfigure the ranking algorithm to prioritize calculated relevance scores as the primary ranking factor, fixing Product Discovery, Typo Tolerance, and Brand Search failures.

Critical Quick Win
2

Reduce sponsored result boost when relevance gaps are significant

Implement relevance thresholds that prevent sponsored products from overriding organic results when relevance score differences exceed 0.15 points.

Critical Quick Win
3

Add Swedish beauty terminology synonym mapping

Create synonym dictionaries connecting Swedish terms like "sminkbas" to "foundation", "hårbalsam" to "conditioner", and "doft" to "fragrance" to fix Language Understanding failures.

Critical Medium Effort
4

Implement product category awareness for sponsored placement

Prevent deodorants from ranking above perfumes in "parfym" searches and ensure product type matching takes precedence over sponsored boosts.

Critical Medium Effort
5

Add price-aware ranking for budget qualifiers

Boost lower-priced products when queries contain terms like "billig" (cheap) while maintaining relevance as a secondary factor.

Critical Medium Effort
6

Fix compound word and special character handling

Improve parsing of Swedish compound words like "ansiktskräm" and special characters in brand names like "L'Oréal" to ensure proper matching and ranking.

Critical Medium Effort
7

Implement attribute-specific boosting for multi-constraint queries

When customers specify attributes like "waterproof" or "damaged hair", boost products containing those exact attributes over generic matches.

Critical Major Project
Industry Context

How You Compare

Metric Your Score Industry Target Source
Relevant result in top 3 37% 80%+ Baymard Institute
Average best result position #6 #1–2 Industry best practice
Irrelevant #1 result 18% <10% Industry best practice

70% of ecommerce sites cannot handle typos in their site search (Baymard Institute). Sites that do handle them gain a significant edge.

~30% of ecommerce visitors use site search, but they generate a disproportionate share of revenue — search users convert at 1.8–3x the rate of browse-only visitors (Econsultancy, Algolia).

31% of site searches end at a dead end — irrelevant results or zero results — across the average ecommerce site (Baymard Institute).

The top 3 results capture ~55–60% of all clicks. Results below position 3 are seen by a rapidly shrinking share of customers.

Revenue-Risk Failures

The queries most likely to cost sales

These examples show where the customer either sees no useful result, sees the wrong intent first, or has to dig past weaker results to find the right product.

#1 ansiktskrem Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #15, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search engine correctly retrieved relevant face cream products for the synonym query 'ansiktskrem', but severely misranked them. The highest relevance products were buried: Kiehl's Ultra Facial Cream (0.781 relevance) was ranked #15, Clinique Moisture Surge (0.777 relevance) was ranked #14, and COSRX Advanced Snail Cream (0.746 relevance) was ranked #8. Meanwhile, lower relevance products dominated the top positions: Dr. Althea Relief Cream (0.656 relevance) was ranked #1, and SKIN1004 Sun serum (0.494 relevance) was ranked #3.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

Customer saw

#1 0.66

Dr. Althea 345 Relief Cream 50 ml 287,20 kr

#2 0.71

Klairs Rich Moist Soothing Cream 80 ml 243,20 kr

#3 0.49

SKIN1004 Madagascar Centella Hyalu-Cica WATER-FIT Sun serum 50 ml 225,60 kr

#4 0.58

La Roche-Posay Balm B5+ 100 ml 164 kr

#5 0.66

Purito Mighty Bamboo Panthenol Cream 100 ml 223,20 kr

Should have seen

#15 0.78

Kiehl's Ultra Facial Cream 28 ml 233,75 kr

#14 0.78

Clinique Moisture Surge 100-Hour Auto-Replenishing Moisturizing Face Cream 50 ml 310,40 kr

#8 0.75

COSRX Advanced Snail 92 All in one Cream 100 ml 180,80 kr

#12 0.75

CeraVe Facial Moisturising Lotion 52 ml 120 kr

#13 0.74

CeraVe Facial Moisturising Lotion SPF50 52 ml 135,20 kr

#2 shampo Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #14, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search engine successfully found relevant shampoo products despite the typo 'shampo', but ranked them poorly. The highest relevance result 'Artègo Good Society 05 Ash Blue Shampo 250 ml' (0.766 relevance) was buried at position #14, while lower-relevance results like 'Loreal Paris Elvital Dream Length Shampoo 250 ml' (0.688 relevance) appeared at #1. Additionally, 'milk_shake No Frizz Allowed Perfecting Shampo 300 ml' (0.758 relevance) was at #6, and 'Alfons Åberg Alfons duschtvål och shampo 200 ml' (0.719 relevance) was at #15.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#3 l'oréal Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #14, after weaker results.

Evidence

The three highest relevance L'Oréal products were buried deep in results: 'L'Oréal Professionnel Metal DX Mask 250 ml' (0.734 relevance) was ranked #14, 'L'Oréal Professionnel Tecni.Art Flex Pli Spray 190 ml' (0.730 relevance) was ranked #5, and 'L'Oréal Professionnel Metal DX Serie Expert Shampoo 300 ml' (0.723 relevance) was ranked #10. Meanwhile, lower-relevance products like 'Loreal Paris True Match Super-Blendable Foundation 1.N' (0.668 relevance) and 'Loreal Paris Elvital Growth Booster Hair Serum' (0.664 relevance) occupied the top positions.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#4 shampoo Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #13, after weaker results.

Evidence

The highest relevance result 'IDA WARG Beauty Everyday Shampoo 100 ml' (0.816 relevance) was buried at position #13, while lower-relevance sponsored results occupied the top positions. The top 3 positions contained results with relevance scores of 0.801, 0.781, and 0.789, but the best match was displaced 12 positions down.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#5 foundation Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #13, after weaker results.

Evidence

The highest relevance foundation product 'Loreal Paris Infaillible 32H Fresh Wear Foundation' (0.777 relevance) was buried at position #13, while a sponsored setting powder with only 0.379 relevance occupied the #1 spot. Multiple high-relevance foundation products were displaced: True Match foundations (0.773 relevance) at positions #3 and #15, and Maybelline foundations (0.754-0.766 relevance) scattered throughout positions #5, #6, #10, #11.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#6 schampo Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #13, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search engine correctly retrieved relevant shampoo products for the Swedish spelling 'schampo', but severely misranked them. The three most relevant results were buried deep: 'Barnängen Fukt&Glans Schampo' (0.758 relevance) was ranked #13, 'Barnängen Normal Schampo Ekologiska Örter' (0.719 relevance) was ranked #8, and 'Barnängen Närande Schampo Kokosnöt' (0.711 relevance) was ranked #9. Meanwhile, sponsored results with lower relevance scores (0.648) occupied the top two positions.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

Detailed Analysis

Product Discovery

5 examples
"shampoo"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #13

The highest relevance result 'IDA WARG Beauty Everyday Shampoo 100 ml' (0.816 relevance) was buried at position #13, while lower-relevance sponsored results occupied the top positions. The top 3 positions contained results with relevance scores of 0.801, 0.781, and 0.789, but the best match was displaced 12 positions down.

Customer Saw

  1. Sponsrad Neccin Anti-Dandruff shampoo 1000 ml (503,20 kr)
  2. Sponsrad Waterclouds Detox Shampoo 250 ml (175,20 kr)
  3. Redken All Soft Shampoo 500 ml (285,60 kr)
  4. Loreal Paris Elvital Collagen Lifter Shampoo 250 ml (32,13 kr)
  5. L'Oréal Professionnel Metal DX Serie Expert Shampoo 300 ml (289,60 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. IDA WARG Beauty Everyday Shampoo 100 ml (49 kr)
  2. Sponsrad Neccin Anti-Dandruff shampoo 1000 ml (503,20 kr)
  3. L'Oréal Professionnel Metal DX Serie Expert Shampoo 300 ml (289,60 kr)

Best match was buried at position #13.

"foundation"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #13

The highest relevance foundation product 'Loreal Paris Infaillible 32H Fresh Wear Foundation' (0.777 relevance) was buried at position #13, while a sponsored setting powder with only 0.379 relevance occupied the #1 spot. Multiple high-relevance foundation products were displaced: True Match foundations (0.773 relevance) at positions #3 and #15, and Maybelline foundations (0.754-0.766 relevance) scattered throughout positions #5, #6, #10, #11.

  1. Sponsrad Make Up Store Hydra Silk Setting Powder Transparent (223,20 kr)

"naglar"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #12

The search engine correctly retrieved nail products for the 'naglar' category query, but severely misranked them. The highest relevance product 'Essie Nail Lacquer 13 Mademoiselle' (0.471 relevance) was buried at position #12, while much lower relevance products like 'Depend Gel Builder Soft Pink' (0.295 relevance) and 'Depend Gel Builder Startkit' (0.293 relevance) occupied the top positions. The top 3 results had an average relevance of only 0.342, while the actual top 3 most relevant products averaged 0.450.

Customer Saw

  1. Depend Gel Builder Soft Pink (74,25 kr)
  2. Depend Gel Builder Startkit (313,50 kr)
  3. Depend Gel iQ Gel Nail Polish French Pink (66,75 kr)
  4. Depend PT Peelingpenna (59,25 kr)
  5. Depend Gel Builder Topcoat (66,75 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Essie Nail Lacquer 13 Mademoiselle (101,50 kr)
  2. Depend Gel iQ Gel Nail Polish French Pink (66,75 kr)
  3. Essie On-a-roll Apricot Nail and Cuticle Oil 13,5 ml (115,50 kr)

Best match was buried at position #12.

"balsam"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #6

All 15 results are generic conditioners and shampoos with no connection to 'balsam': Löwengrip shampoo (#1), By Lyko volume products (#2), L'Oreal conditioners (#3, #8, #9, #13, #14), Waterclouds conditioner (#4), etc. None contain 'balsam' in their titles despite this being a direct product name search.

Customer Saw

  1. Sponsrad Löwengrip The Cure Repair & Shine Shampoo 250 ml (159,20 kr)
  2. Sponsrad By Lyko Plump It Up Volume Shampoo 250 ml & Conditioner 250 ml (179 kr)
  3. Loreal Paris Elvital Glycolic Gloss Ultimate Conditioner For Dull Hair 150 ml (69,30 kr)
  4. Waterclouds Moist Conditioner 200 ml (175,20 kr)

Typo Tolerance

5 examples
"shampo"
Typo · Critical · Best result at position #14

The search engine successfully found relevant shampoo products despite the typo 'shampo', but ranked them poorly. The highest relevance result 'Artègo Good Society 05 Ash Blue Shampo 250 ml' (0.766 relevance) was buried at position #14, while lower-relevance results like 'Loreal Paris Elvital Dream Length Shampoo 250 ml' (0.688 relevance) appeared at #1. Additionally, 'milk_shake No Frizz Allowed Perfecting Shampo 300 ml' (0.758 relevance) was at #6, and 'Alfons Åberg Alfons duschtvål och shampo 200 ml' (0.719 relevance) was at #15.

Language Understanding

4 examples
"ansiktskrem"
Synonym · Critical · Best result at position #15

The search engine correctly retrieved relevant face cream products for the synonym query 'ansiktskrem', but severely misranked them. The highest relevance products were buried: Kiehl's Ultra Facial Cream (0.781 relevance) was ranked #15, Clinique Moisture Surge (0.777 relevance) was ranked #14, and COSRX Advanced Snail Cream (0.746 relevance) was ranked #8. Meanwhile, lower relevance products dominated the top positions: Dr. Althea Relief Cream (0.656 relevance) was ranked #1, and SKIN1004 Sun serum (0.494 relevance) was ranked #3.

"sminkbas"
Synonym · Critical · Best result at position #2

The query 'sminkbas' (Swedish for 'foundation') returned makeup accessories like beauty bags, powder puffs, and brushes instead of foundation products. None of the top 15 results appear to be actual foundation/base makeup products. The highest relevance score was only 0.436, and results include items like 'By Lyko Padded Beauty Bag Pink' (#1), 'By Lyko Cushion Cloud Mini Puffs' (#3), and 'Real Techniques Mini Miracle Powder Puff Trio' (#4) - all accessories rather than foundation products.

  2. Kérastase Resistance Bain Force Architecte shampoo 250 ml (307,20 kr)
  3. Kérastase Chronologiste Bain Régénérant Shampoo 250 ml (376 kr)
  4. Kérastase Resistance Resistance Bain Force Architecte Shampoo Refill 500 ml (449,60 kr)
  5. Kérastase Resistance Fondant Extentioniste conditioner 200 ml (432 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Kérastase Première Premìere Discovery Set for damaged hair (528 kr)
  2. Kérastase Resistance Ciment Anti-Usure Topseal conditioner 200 ml (388 kr)
  3. Kérastase Resistance Fondant Extentioniste conditioner 200 ml (432 kr)

Best match was buried at position #8.

"maybelline mascara svart vattentät"
Multi-attribute · Moderate · Best result at position #1

The search correctly retrieved relevant Maybelline waterproof mascaras, but the ranking is suboptimal. High-relevance waterproof products are buried: 'Maybelline New York The Colossal Mascara Waterproof Black' (0.855 relevance) appears at position #9 instead of #2, 'Maybelline New York Lash Sensational Firework Waterproof Mascara Very Black' (0.828 relevance) appears at position #13 instead of #4, and 'Maybelline New York Lash Sensational Sky High Mascara Black Waterproof Duo' (0.824 relevance) appears at position #15 instead of #5. Meanwhile, non-waterproof products like 'Maybelline New York Lash Sensational Mascara 01 Very Black' (0.789 relevance) rank higher at position #2.

Customer Saw

  1. Maybelline New York Sensational Mascara Very Black Waterproof Sensational Mascara Black Waterproof (122,50 kr)
  2. Maybelline New York Lash Sensational Mascara 01 Very Black (122,50 kr)
  3. Maybelline New York Lash Sensational Body Waterproof Mascara Very Black (132,30 kr)
  4. Maybelline New York Falsies Surreal Extensions Waterprof Mascara (139,30 kr)
  5. Maybelline New York Lash Sensational Full Fan Effect Mascara Intense Black (125,30 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. Maybelline New York Sensational Mascara Very Black Waterproof Sensational Mascara Black Waterproof (122,50 kr)
  2. Maybelline New York The Colossal Mascara Waterproof Black (90,30 kr)
  3. Maybelline New York Lash Sensational Body Waterproof Mascara Very Black (132,30 kr)
"cerave ansiktskräm känslig hud"
Multi-attribute · Minor · Best result at position #1

The search correctly retrieved relevant CeraVe facial moisturizers, but several high-relevance products were poorly positioned: 'CeraVe Facial Moisturising Lotion SPF30 52 ml' (relevance 0.691) was at position #6 instead of #2, 'CeraVe Facial Moisturising Lotion SPF50 52 ml' (relevance 0.688) was at position #7 instead of #4, and 'CeraVe Facial Moisturizing Lotion PM Duo 2x52ml' (relevance 0.684) was buried at position #13 instead of #6.

Customer Saw

  1. CeraVe Facial Moisturising Lotion 52 ml (120 kr)
  2. CeraVe Daily Moisturising Cream 50 ml (63,20 kr)
  3. CeraVe Daily Moisturising Cream 340 g (132 kr)
  4. CeraVe SA Smoothing Cream 177 ml (135,20 kr)
  5. CeraVe Daily Moisturising Lotion 473 ml (125,60 kr)

Should Have Seen

  1. CeraVe Facial Moisturising Lotion 52 ml (120 kr)
  2. CeraVe Facial Moisturising Lotion SPF30 52 ml (132 kr)
  3. CeraVe Daily Moisturising Cream 50 ml (63,20 kr)
Positive Findings

What's Working

  • Retrieval is working — relevant results were found in 43 of 60 queries. The primary issue is ranking, not finding: the right results exist in the result set but aren't being surfaced at the top.
  • Close to correct on 21 queries ("mascara", "herrprodukter", "ansiktskräm"): the best result was within the top 3, just not at #1.
  • Your search correctly interprets typos and spelling variations — when we searched with misspellings, it recognized what customers meant. The issue is ranking the corrected results, not recognising the typo.
Full Data

Scope and Boundaries

What this audit covers

Included

Search relevance, ranking quality, retrieval gaps, query interpretation failures, visible result ordering, and prioritized fixes based on the tested query set.

Not included

Implementation work, platform configuration changes, merchandising strategy, analytics instrumentation, or a full conversion-rate analysis beyond the search result experience.

Next Steps

We'd welcome the opportunity to walk you through these findings in detail and discuss a remediation plan tailored to your team's priorities and timeline.

The issues identified in this audit are fixable — most ecommerce search platforms support the capabilities tested here, and the roadmap above is ordered by impact to help you prioritize.

Schedule a 30-Minute Walkthrough
